creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Mark Millard from comment #6)

I will note that:

"Two types have compatible type if there types are the same."

but there are additional specialized criteria so

"Two types need not be identical to be compatible."

I'm not sure that the words are explicit about the specifics of compatibility for the context.


But it is likely easiest to think of:

pointer to unsigned short is not compatible with pointer to int because unsigned short vs. int are not (sufficiently) compatible.

In other words the compatibility criteria here would involve stack-memory layout compatibility: calling-code generation based on just seeing the prototype must be compatible with with the function implementation based on K&R C style default argument-promotion.

[The function definition is the same no matter if the forward declaration's prototype is available or not at the definition.]
